journeys.title: Verify Kueue scheduling columns visible in deployments table
journeys.source: Inferred from AC-1: Kueue-specific scheduling state displays in table

steps.narration: Navigated to Model Deployments overview.
Verified table state. Headers include Queue and Scheduling columns. 6 deployment rows visible with Kueue scheduling labels (Admitted, Running, Pending, Suspended, Scaling, Unmanaged).
Captured default table view showing all 6 deployments with Kueue scheduling states.

journeys.title: Verify no Kueue indicators when feature disabled
journeys.source: Inferred from AC-2: No Kueue-related indicators when disabled

steps.narration: Navigated to Model Deployments overview.
Source confirms enableKueueScheduling flag gates Queue and Scheduling columns. Current state shows Kueue enabled.
Hovered over Running status label for unique screenshot evidence.
Captured table with Kueue columns visible. Cannot demonstrate disabled state without toggling feature flag.

journeys.title: Verify unmanaged deployment shows normal status
journeys.source: Inferred from AC-3: InferenceService with no Workload CR displays normal status

steps.narration: Navigated to Model Deployments overview.
Project Y Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct row shows Ready status and Unmanaged scheduling label — no Kueue indicators.
Hovered over Pending status label for unique screenshot.
Captured table showing unmanaged deployment alongside Kueue-managed ones.

journeys.title: Verify no error indicators for RBAC-limited user
journeys.source: Inferred from AC-5: No error indicators when lacking RBAC permission

steps.narration: Navigated to Model Deployments overview.
Hovered over Suspended status label to trigger tooltip.
Tooltip shows resource allocation. No 403 errors, no broken UI elements. Page renders cleanly.
Captured clean page with Suspended tooltip visible — no error indicators present.

journeys.title: Verify resource tooltip on scheduling status hover
journeys.source: Inferred from AC-6: Tooltip displays CPU, memory, GPU resources on hover

steps.narration: Navigated to Model Deployments overview.
Hovered over Admitted status label for Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct to trigger resource tooltip.
Tooltip displays: GPU: 1 requested / 1 admitted, CPU: 4 requested / 4 admitted, Memory: 16Gi requested / 16Gi admitted.
Captured Admitted label tooltip showing GPU, CPU, Memory resource allocation.

journeys.title: Verify expandable row with queue details and resource allocation
journeys.source: Inferred from AC-1, AC-7: Queue details panel with resource breakdown

steps.narration: Navigated to Model Deployments overview.
Expanded Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct row to reveal queue details panel.
Expanded content shows Queue Details (gpu-inference-queue, gpu-cluster-queue, high-priority), Resource Allocation (GPU/CPU/Memory), and Timing sections.
Captured expanded row showing Queue Details, Resource Allocation, and Timing panels.

journeys.title: Verify coverage across deployment types with expanded details
journeys.source: Inferred from AC-7: Integration covers both InferenceService and LLMInferenceService

steps.narration: Navigated to Model Deployments overview.
Expanded Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 row to verify second deployment type.
Expanded content shows Queue Details (gpu-inference-queue, default priority), Resource Allocation (2 GPU, 8 CPU, 32Gi), and Timing.
Captured expanded row for second deployment type confirming coverage across resource types.

persona_selection.reasoning: Target audience includes platform operators and ML engineers managing model deployments with Kueue scheduling. Maude personas (MLOps) cover both experience levels.

usability_dimensions.persona_selection: Two variants of the MLOps persona (Maude) — experienced (strong K8s/Kueue knowledge) and junior (basic K8s only) — to test whether Kueue-specific terminology creates comprehension gaps across experience levels.

maude-experienced.finding: All tasks completable on a single page with expandable rows and tooltips. No navigation required beyond the initial table view.

maude-junior.finding: Can complete tasks on the single page but encounters unfamiliar Kueue terminology (Admitted, Workload CR) that creates hesitation without blocking completion.

maude-experienced.finding: All tasks completable on a single page with expandable rows and tooltips. No navigation required beyond the initial table view.

maude-junior.finding: Can complete tasks on the single page but encounters unfamiliar Kueue terminology (Admitted, Workload CR) that creates hesitation without blocking completion.

dimensions.name: Cross-Persona Handoffs

maude-experienced.finding: N/A — this is a single-user read-only viewing feature with no handoff between personas.

maude-junior.finding: N/A — no cross-persona handoff exists in this feature.

dimensions.na_reason: Single-user read-only viewing feature with no workflow handoff between personas.

maude-experienced.finding: N/A — this is a single-user read-only viewing feature with no handoff between personas.

maude-junior.finding: N/A — no cross-persona handoff exists in this feature.

maude-experienced.finding: Progressive disclosure works well: table-level summary → tooltip details → expanded row with full queue hierarchy. Expert details accessible without blocking basic view.

maude-junior.finding: Basic table view is usable but expanded row details (ClusterQueue, PriorityClass hierarchy) may overwhelm users without Kueue knowledge.

maude-experienced.finding: Progressive disclosure works well: table-level summary → tooltip details → expanded row with full queue hierarchy. Expert details accessible without blocking basic view.

maude-junior.finding: Basic table view is usable but expanded row details (ClusterQueue, PriorityClass hierarchy) may overwhelm users without Kueue knowledge.

maude-experienced.finding: Status labels are accurate and informative. Tooltips show real resource data (requested vs admitted). Expanded rows provide full queue context.

maude-junior.finding: Status labels are visible but 'Admitted' and 'Suspended' are confusing without context. Junior doesn't know what state transitions to expect.

maude-experienced.finding: Status labels are accurate and informative. Tooltips show real resource data (requested vs admitted). Expanded rows provide full queue context.

maude-junior.finding: Status labels are visible but 'Admitted' and 'Suspended' are confusing without context. Junior doesn't know what state transitions to expect.

maude-experienced.finding: Shows exactly the right level of K8s/Kueue detail for an operator. Queue hierarchy, resource allocation, and timing are all relevant signals.

maude-junior.finding: Too much infrastructure terminology exposed without explanation. ClusterQueue, Workload CR, admission states are noise for basic K8s users.

maude-experienced.finding: Shows exactly the right level of K8s/Kueue detail for an operator. Queue hierarchy, resource allocation, and timing are all relevant signals.

maude-junior.finding: Too much infrastructure terminology exposed without explanation. ClusterQueue, Workload CR, admission states are noise for basic K8s users.

maude-experienced.finding: Speaks the operator's language perfectly. LocalQueue→ClusterQueue→PriorityClass hierarchy maps directly to Kueue architecture.

maude-junior.finding: Kueue-specific terms (Admitted, ClusterQueue, Workload CR) don't match junior's mental model of basic K8s. Labels like 'Unmanaged' read as potentially broken rather than intentionally external.

maude-experienced.finding: Speaks the operator's language perfectly. LocalQueue→ClusterQueue→PriorityClass hierarchy maps directly to Kueue architecture.

maude-junior.finding: Kueue-specific terms (Admitted, ClusterQueue, Workload CR) don't match junior's mental model of basic K8s. Labels like 'Unmanaged' read as potentially broken rather than intentionally external.

maude-experienced.finding: PatternFly components provide baseline accessibility (semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation). Cannot fully verify without screen reader testing.

maude-junior.finding: Same PatternFly baseline applies. Tooltips on hover may not be accessible via keyboard-only interaction without additional testing.

maude-experienced.finding: PatternFly components provide baseline accessibility (semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation). Cannot fully verify without screen reader testing.

maude-junior.finding: Same PatternFly baseline applies. Tooltips on hover may not be accessible via keyboard-only interaction without additional testing.

confusion_events: Tooltip references 'Workload CR' — unknown Custom Resource type
Label 'Suspended' in Kueue context is ambiguous for basic K8s user
'Unmanaged' label reads as misconfigured rather than intentionally external to queue system
'ClusterQueue' is a Kueue-specific CRD not in basic K8s vocabulary
'PriorityClass' in Kueue context differs from K8s pod PriorityClass

traces.narration_summary: Experienced Maude navigated directly to deployments, immediately recognized Kueue scheduling states, hovered for resource tooltips, and expanded the row for queue hierarchy details. Zero friction — the UI speaks her language perfectly.

Queue and scheduling columns are immediately visible in the table without extra clicks. Experienced Maude confirmed managed vs unmanaged differentiation and found no error indicators — clean RBAC handling.

Expanded row provides comprehensive resource allocation (requested vs admitted for GPU/CPU/Memory) and queue position (LocalQueue→ClusterQueue→PriorityClass). Matches what she'd see from kubectl describe workload.

Junior Maude found the deployments page but was confused by 'Admitted' status (not standard K8s), 'Workload CR' in tooltips, and 'Suspended' label. Could piece together that a Pending deployment needs resources, but the Kueue terminology created significant cognitive friction.

Junior Maude confirmed scheduling/queue info is visible but was confused by 'Unmanaged' label — initially read it as potentially misconfigured rather than intentionally external to queue management. Recovered after seeing Ready status with no errors.

Junior Maude expanded a row and encountered ClusterQueue and PriorityClass (Kueue-specific CRDs). Resource allocation numbers (requested vs admitted) were understandable, but the queue hierarchy assumed Kueue-specific knowledge she doesn't have.
